title: Fever
uae_applicability: Verified for Dubai and DHA-licensed telehealth only; broader UAE applicability is not asserted.

source_references.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Common Cold

sections.heading: 1. Background
sections.evidence_summary: Identifies severity-risk contexts and distinguishes uncomplicated common cold from other respiratory presentations.

sections.heading: 2. Scope; 3. Purpose; 4. Applicability
sections.evidence_summary: Defines Dubai telehealth scope and emergency/controlled-medication exclusions.

sections.heading: 6. Red Flags
sections.evidence_summary: Lists respiratory, swallowing, neurologic, rash, and pediatric breathing concerns that require assessment or escalation.

source_references.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Fever in Children

sections.heading: 1. Background
sections.evidence_summary: Defines pediatric fever assessment context and the importance of age, measurement site, appearance, persistence, and underlying disease.

sections.heading: 2. Scope; 3. Purpose; 4. Applicability
sections.evidence_summary: Defines Dubai telehealth pediatric fever scope and emergency exclusions.

sections.heading: 5.1 Virtual Clinical Assessment
sections.evidence_summary: Prioritises airway, breathing, circulation, consciousness, age-specific risk, and illness-severity assessment.

sections.heading: 7. Referral Criteria
sections.evidence_summary: Uses risk features to guide emergency referral, specialist assessment, or explicitly documented safety-netting.

evidence_links.direct_relationship: The reviewed 1. Background section is directly relevant to documenting Fever, subject to the recorded population and setting limitations.

unresolved_source_gaps: No single exact source covers the mixed-age, combined fever/URTI workflow across telehealth and in-person settings.
Qualified clinician review has not been completed.

All previously supported GP legacy-item mappings were conservatively removed because exact item identity, direct support, and item-specific applicability were not independently established under the fail-closed contract.

items.text: antipyretic use as per clinician plan
